task_completion.description: The agent opened the correct patient's chart in LibreHealth EHR.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreHealth EHR interface is visible with the target patient's name (matching the contents of /tmp/target_patient.txt) displayed in the active patient banner or header.

task_completion.description: The Penicillin allergy is recorded with the correct title and type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An allergy entry or list shows 'Penicillin' as the allergen/title and 'Allergy' as the type.

task_completion.description: The Penicillin allergy includes the correct reaction, severity, and onset date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Penicillin allergy entry explicitly shows Reaction: 'Rash', Severity: 'Moderate', and Onset Date: '2015-06-01'.

task_completion.description: The Sulfonamides allergy is recorded with the correct title and type.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An allergy entry or list shows 'Sulfonamides' (or 'Sulfa') as the allergen/title and 'Allergy' as the type.

task_completion.description: The Sulfonamides allergy includes the correct reaction, severity, and onset date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sulfonamides/Sulfa allergy entry explicitly shows Reaction: 'Anaphylaxis', Severity: 'Severe', and Onset Date: '2020-11-15'.

task_completion.description: Both allergies are successfully saved and visible in the patient's medical record summary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient's main chart, dashboard, or active allergies list displays both the Penicillin and Sulfonamides records as saved entries, not just unsaved form drafts.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreHealth EHR web interface to enter the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and LibreHealth EHR GUI forms, rather than terminal commands executing SQL queries to insert the data.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual LibreHealth EHR application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface is clearly LibreHealth EHR (menus, patient banners, medical forms) and not a text editor or generic note-taking app.

integrity.description: The agent entered the allergies for an existing patient rather than creating a dummy patient.
integrity.visual_evidence: The patient banner shows a pre-existing patient record (part of the NHANES dataset) rather than a newly created patient with a generic name.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Allergy 1 must be 'Penicillin' with Type: Allergy, Reaction: Rash, Severity: Moderate, Onset Date: 2015-06-01. Allergy 2 must be 'Sulfonamides' (or 'Sulfa') with Type: Allergy, Reaction: Anaphylaxis, Severity: Severe, Onset Date: 2020-11-15. The system uses real NHANES patient data.
